selected_important_turn.text: Sure, I'll take 3 food and 1 water would be nice. you can have 2 waters and all of the firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn locks in the concrete allocation that resolves the negotiation. It determines how scarce items (especially water) are split and thus strongly shapes both parties' outcomes and the final deal.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although cooperative, it's suboptimal for YOU given your stated preferences: you value water highly but concede two waters to the other party. That generosity unnecessarily sacrifices your own high-priority item and misses a chance to trade lower-value items (firewood) for more water or other compensation.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Signal your own priorities or make a conditional trade: propose keeping more water in exchange for giving up the low-value item (firewood) or offer to take food only if you retain an extra water. Frame it as a small adjustment (e.g., ask for one more water or another form of compensation) rather than a demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: slightly increase — by enabling a trade that better fits both parties' values (more efficient allocation of items each values most).
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — you retain more of what you value while the other still gets most of their priorities, so the split is more balanced relative to true preferences.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: slightly decrease short-term (may require a brief small negotiation), but overall similar — a modest request framed cooperatively is unlikely to block agreement.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: maintain or improve — asking politely about a small adjustment demonstrates honesty about needs while still being cooperative.

value2reason.Medium: We already have food, but it could be for snacks.
value2reason.High: We can use the water for extra hikes.

value2reason.Low: Food is good when you are hungry.
value2reason.Medium: Always nice to have a fire.
value2reason.High: Need lots of water when hiking.

dialogue.text: Hello hood to see a fellow camper.  How are you doing today?🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hey! I'm doing great, how are you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Looking forward to doing some serious hiking with the family.  bad luck on the way here we flew in and the airline loss or bags with some of our supplies and sleeping bags.☹️
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: oh nooo, that's terrible. do they know where they left them?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think they went to Chicago.  I really needing replace our lost water and sleeping bags.  I see there are some extra supplies to share.  I could really use the water and firwood.  🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: oh yeah, you definitely do. we have all of our stuff and mostly wanted to hike and have picnics during our camping trip
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Great. If you mainly doing picnics do you want 3 Food?   I could use the the supplies.  🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Sure, I'll take 3 food and 1 water would be nice. you can have 2 waters and all of the firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'd like to adjust the water split. Let's say I take 2 waters, and you take 1. In exchange, I'll give you 1 firewood package. That way, I keep more of my high-value items while still being fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need more water for the hike. How about I take 2 waters, 1 firewood, and you take 1 water, 2 firewood? That way we both get what we need without sacrificing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that split. Let me confirm: you take 2 waters, 1 firewood, and I take 1 water, 2 firewood. That works for me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. Let me confirm the split: you take 2 waters, 1 firewood, and I take 1 water, 2 firewood. All packages accounted for. Ready to finalize.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Actually, let me clarify. I meant to take 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood, while you take 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood. This better aligns with my priorities and ensures a fairer split for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see. Let me check the numbers again. If I take 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood, that leaves 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood for you. That adds up to all 3 of each item. I’m okay with this split. Let’s finalize it.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Final split confirmed: I take 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood. You take 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood. All packages accounted for. Let’s wrap this up.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
